shower.thought: We spend years learning how to read other people's thoughts through books, but we never learn how to organize our own.
Time moves faster the better you're doing, which means suffering is the only honest measurement of how long anything takes.
You can't remember being born, which means your life actually started in the middle of the story.
The things you're most afraid to say are usually the things everyone else is afraid to say too.
Confidence is just deciding that your embarrassment isn't worth more than your goal.
You only realize a song is stuck in your head after you've been humming it, which means you've already lost the battle.
Every conversation you have is with a version of that person that only exists in your head.
The worst part about growing up is realizing that adults don't have a secret manual—they're just guessing louder than everyone else.
You can't taste your own saliva until you think about it, and now you're thinking about it.
People say "I have nothing to hide" but they close the browser tab when someone walks by, which tells you everything.
The difference between a hobby and an obsession is just other people's permission.
You apologize for things you didn't do wrong all the time, which means you've already decided the relationship is worth more than being right.
Forgetting something is just your brain deciding it wasn't important enough to keep, and you have no say in it.
The older you get, the faster time moves, but also the slower you move, so you're basically experiencing time twice.
We call it "falling asleep" but we're actually just turning off.
You can't un-hear a song that's stuck in your head, which means some part of you is always someone else's jukebox.
The scariest part about having a choice is that you could always choose differently, and you'll never know what would've happened if you did.
Everyone's crazy, they just hide it really well until they find someone else who's crazy in the same way.
You judge yourself on your intentions but everyone else on their actions, and somehow you think that's fair.
If you've ever felt like an imposter in your own life, congratulations—you're more self-aware than most.

motivational.thought: Your limitation to start is just a limitation in your mind. — Unknown
The only thing standing between you and your goals is the story you keep telling yourself about why you can't achieve them. — Jordan Belfort
Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most. — Abraham Lincoln
You miss 100% of the shots you don't take. — Wayne Gretzky
The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now. — Chinese Proverb
Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts. — Winston Churchill
Your future is created by what you do today, not tomorrow. — Unknown
The only person you should try to be better than is the person you were yesterday. — Unknown
Comfort is the enemy of progress. — Jocko Willink
Doubt kills more dreams than failure ever will. — Unknown
What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
You don't rise to the level of your goals; you fall to the level of your systems. — James Clear
The cave you fear to enter holds the treasure you seek. — Joseph Campbell
Most people overestimate what they can do in a year and underestimate what they can do in a decade. — Bill Gates
Your potential is endless. Your excuses are not. — Unknown
Regret for the things we did is temporary, but regret for the things we didn't do is permanent. — Unknown
The only way to do great work is to love what you do. — Steve Jobs
Every master was once a beginner who refused to give up. — Unknown
Suffering teaches you how to live; avoiding it teaches you nothing. — Unknown
Your life is not waiting for you to find your purpose. Your purpose is waiting for you to start. — Unknown

darkjoke.thought: My therapist asked why I never finish anything. I said, "Well, let me tell you about my—"
I'm not saying my childhood was bad, but my imaginary friend moved out.
My doctor told me I have six months to live. I said I can't afford the bills. He said, "Take a year."
I tried to make a belt out of herbs once. It was a waist of thyme, but at least I tried something.
People keep asking if I'm okay. No, but I've gotten really good at lying about it.
My GPS said "recalculating" the same way my mom looked at me when I came out of college with a philosophy degree.
I told my boss I needed a mental health day. He said, "You're already working from home." Touché.
My dating profile says "emotionally unavailable." Turns out I was just being honest about my relationship history.
I asked my barber for something that would make me look smarter. He gave me a referral to a therapist.
I used to be addicted to soap, but I'm clean now. Still working on everything else though.
My wife asked what I was thinking about. I said, "Nothing." She was right to be suspicious—I was actually thinking about how penguins have knees.
I told my friend he was drinking too much. He said, "At least I'm consistent." Fair point.
My therapist says I need to work on my self-sabotage. I told her I'd think about it and never went back.
I got a promotion at work. Turns out it just means I fail upward faster.
My ex said she needed space. I gave her the whole apartment and moved to my mom's basement. Still too close.
I'm pretty sure my car's check engine light is just there to keep me company at this point.
My parent asked, "Why can't you be like your cousin?" Because he's in prison, Mom. He's literally not allowed to be anywhere else.
I tried to take my own advice once. Turns out I'm full of shit.
My sleep schedule is so bad, I'm pretty sure my circadian rhythm filed for divorce.
I told someone I was going through a phase. They asked how long it had been. Twenty-three years, actually.
